FT_IC1.evidence: 9254 youth from US ABCD Study (Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development Study)

FT_IC2.evidence: Screen media activity (SMA) operationalized as latent factor from multiple items: TV/movies, videos, video games on computer/console, messaging, social media sites, video chat. Separately measured SMA; qualifying screen media variable. Applies B1 (separate SM-related coefficients in same model).

FT_IC3.evidence: Logistic mixed models testing SM → recreational activity engagement; mixed graphical models with regression framework; multiple ORs reported with CIs.

FT_IC4.evidence: Multiple ORs with 95% CIs reported in abstract and results (e.g., 'adjusted odds ratio = 0.83, 95% CI 0.76–0.89' for games/movies; 'soccer (0.93, 0.88–0.98)', 'baseball (0.92, 0.86–0.98)').

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical ABCD analysis with SM as predictor of non-SM outcomes (recreational activities).

FT_IC6.evidence: Published research article (2020, BMC Public Health); no evidence of preprint/abstract duplicate.

FT_EC7_reason: Paper does NOT meet EC7 criteria. SM is the INDEPENDENT variable predicting recreational activity engagement (non-SM outcome), not DV. EC7 requires SM to be DV with demographic-only predictors.
notes: SMA operationalized as latent factor with separate measurement from other constructs. Uses mixed models framework. Cross-sectional design.

FT_IC1.evidence: 9986 families from ABCD Study (Adolescent Brain and Cognitive Development Study); preadolescents aged 9–10 years

FT_IC2.evidence: Screen time with multiple types measured: TV/movie, video, video game, texting, social media, video chatting, mature video game, R-rated movie. Age-inappropriate screen time as primary exposure. Qualifying screen media and social media variables (texting on phone, social media use). Applies B1 (separate per-item coefficients).

FT_IC3.evidence: Mixed graphical models (network analysis) estimating conditional dependencies and edge weights between nodes; multiple regression framework with significance testing (permutation-based tests).

FT_IC4.evidence: Network edge weights and regression coefficients reported in tables and figures; Table 1 shows descriptive statistics; network connectivity metrics reported.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical ABCD analysis with screen time as predictor of mood symptom outcomes.

FT_IC6.evidence: Published research article (2020, Journal of Abnormal Child Psychology); no evidence of duplicate.

FT_EC7_reason: Paper does NOT meet EC7 criteria. Screen time is the INDEPENDENT variable predicting mood symptoms (outcome), not the DV. EC7 requires SM/ST to be DV with demographic-only predictors. Additionally, BIS/BAS and parental monitoring are included as predictors (non-demographic factors disqualify per B17).
notes: Uses network psychometric approach with mixed graphical models. Cross-sectional design. Multiple screen time types examined separately.

FT_IC1.evidence: Cross-sectional study includes children from Dutch ABCD Study (n=2,952) AND Spanish INMA Project (n=261). NOT US ABCD only; pooled analysis with non-US ABCD samples. Per binding rule: Amsterdam/Dutch ABCD disqualifies.

FT_IC2.evidence: Primary exposure is radiofrequency electromagnetic fields (RF-EMF), not qualifying smartphone/SM variable. Screen time assessed but RF-EMF is main independent variable of interest. Per B5: RF-EMF → NOT_MET for FT-IC2.

FT_IC3.evidence: Not evaluated due to FT-IC1 NOT_MET and FT-IC2 NOT_MET failures.

FT_IC4.evidence: Not evaluated due to FT-IC1 NOT_MET and FT-IC2 NOT_MET failures.

FT_IC5.evidence: Not evaluated due to FT-IC1 NOT_MET and FT-IC2 NOT_MET failures.

FT_IC6.evidence: Not evaluated due to upstream failures.

FT_EC7_reason: Paper fails FT-IC1 (not US ABCD only) and FT-IC2 (RF-EMF is main exposure, not qualifying SM/ST variable). EC7 not applicable.
notes: EXCLUDED: FT-IC1 NOT_MET (Dutch ABCD Study pooled with INMA non-ABCD sample). FT-IC2 NOT_MET (RF-EMF is primary exposure; screen time is secondary variable only, with RF-EMF focus). This is an environmental epidemiology abstract conference paper on RF-EMF, not SM/ST.
